message:
test(e2e,validation): add Text wrapping E2E tests, fix Tree ASCII guides

- Add comprehensive E2E tests for Text wrapping (41 tests):
  - Basic word wrap and narrow width scenarios
  - Long word breaking
  - Styled text wrapping with span preservation
  - Text.divide() algorithm validation
  - Justification methods (left, right, center)
  - Wide character handling (CJK, emoji)
  - Overflow methods (crop, ellipsis, fold, ignore)
  - Snapshot tests for regression detection

- Fix Tree ASCII guide characters to match Python Rich:
  - Changed branch guide from "|-- " to "+-- "
  - Matches Python Rich's ASCII_GUIDES specification

- Add Tree edge case tests (7 new tests):
  - Empty root, single node
  - Wide Unicode (CJK) labels
  - Emoji labels
  - All guide style variants (bold, double, rounded)

Closes rich_rust-ag4, rich_rust-wfq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@
 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a62","title":"Verify Style Parsing Edge Cases","description":"## Objective
Ensure Style::parse() handles all edge cases from RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.

## Background
Style parsing converts strings like 'bold red on blue' into Style structs. Edge cases include empty strings, invalid keywords, and complex combinations.

## Technical Details

### Test Cases from Spec

#### Valid Inputs
- '' (empty) → null style
- 'bold' → bold attribute only
- 'red' → red foreground only
- 'on blue' → blue background only
- 'bold italic red on blue' → all combined
- 'not bold' → unset bold
- '#ff0000' → hex color foreground
- 'rgb(255,0,0)' → RGB color foreground
- 'color(128)' → 256-color foreground
- 'link=https://example.com' → hyperlink

#### Invalid Inputs (should error or ignore)
- 'invalid' → unknown keyword
- 'bold bold' → duplicate attribute
- 'on' → incomplete background
- 'red blue' → ambiguous (two foregrounds?)

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each valid input
2. Write test for each invalid input
3. Compare error handling to Python Rich
4. Verify error messages are helpful

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (parse method)
- Python Rich style.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.4","acceptance_criteria":"All edge cases handled correctly. Error messages are helpful.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.886973112-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.941197777-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a96","title":"EPIC: Implementation Refinement & Gap Closure","description":"## Objective
Address identified gaps between current implementation and RICH_SPEC requirements, plus implement missing features discovered during validation.

## Background
During spec validation, several gaps and missing features were identified. This epic tracks all implementation work needed to achieve full spec compliance.

## Scope

### Critical Gaps (Must Fix)
1. LRU caching for color parsing - spec requires caching for performance
2. Text.divide() algorithm - must match Python Rich exactly
3. Ratio distribution algorithm - verify integer rounding behavior
4. Hyperlink support (OSC 8) - may be incomplete

### Potential Gaps (Verify First)
1. Style parsing edge cases
2. Markup parser error recovery
3. Table width collapse algorithm
4. Wide character handling in cell calculations

### Implementation Improvements
1. API ergonomics (builder patterns, From impls)
2. Error messages and handling
3. Thread safety considerations

## Success Criteria
- All RICH_SPEC requirements implemented
- No behavioral differences from Python Rich (for in-scope features)
- Clean, idiomatic Rust code

## Dependencies
- Depends on Spec Validation epic completing to identify all gaps","acceptance_criteria":"All identified gaps closed. Spec compliance verified.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:54.039911917-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:54.039911917-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a96","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:04.859808161-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-ag4","title":"Create E2E Test: Text Wrapping and Justification","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for text wrapping, justification, and the Text.divide() algorithm.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Word Wrap
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_word_wrap() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let text = Text::plain("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
        .wrap(20);
    
    let lines: Vec<_> = text.iter().collect();
    debug!(line_count = lines.len(), "Wrapped text");
    
    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {
        let width = line.cell_length();
        debug!(line = i, width = width, content = %line, "Line detail");
        assert!(width <= 20, "Line {} exceeds width: {}", i, width);
    }
    
    info!("Word wrap test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Long Word Breaking
- Word longer than width
- Verify: Word is broken, not truncated

### Scenario 3: Styled Text Wrapping
- Text with style spans
- Verify: Styles preserved across line breaks

### Scenario 4: Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_text_divide() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());  // 'world' is bold
    
    debug!(original = %text.plain, spans = ?text.spans, "Original text");
    
    let divided = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    
    for (i, part) in divided.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            part = i,
            content = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Verify spans are correctly split
    assert_eq!(divided[0].plain, "hello");
    assert!(divided[0].spans.is_empty(), "First part should have no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[1].plain, " worl");
    assert!(!divided[1].spans.is_empty(), "Second part should have bold span");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[2].plain, "d!");
    assert!(!divided[2].spans.is_empty(), "Third part should have bold span");
    
    info!("Text.divide test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 5: Justification
- Left, Right, Center, Full justify
- Verify: Correct padding/spacing

### Scenario 6: Wide Characters
- CJK text wrapping
- Emoji handling
- Verify: Cell width respected

### Scenario 7: Overflow Methods
- Fold, Crop, Ellipsis
- Verify: Each method works correctly

## Logging Requirements
- Original text and spans
- Wrap width and resulting lines
- Divide offsets and resulting parts
- Span transformations during divide
- Cell width calculations

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_text.rs
- tests/golden/text_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All text scenarios pass. divide() algorithm verified with span preservation.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:03.615570733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:39:58.189072708-05:00","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.972287839-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.099529002-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

#[test]

### Scenario 4: Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_text_divide() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());  // 'world' is bold
    
    debug!(original = %text.plain, spans = ?text.spans, "Original text");
    
    let divided = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    
    for (i, part) in divided.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            part = i,
            content = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Verify spans are correctly split
    assert_eq!(divided[0].plain, "hello");
    assert!(divided[0].spans.is_empty(), "First part should have no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[1].plain, " worl");
    assert!(!divided[1].spans.is_empty(), "Second part should have bold span");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[2].plain, "d!");
    assert!(!divided[2].spans.is_empty(), "Third part should have bold span");
    
    info!("Text.divide test PASSED");
}
```

 {"id":"rich_rust-agw","title":"Add Comprehensive Rustdoc Comments","description":"## Objective
Add complete rustdoc documentation to all public APIs.

## Documentation Standards

### Module Documentation
Each module (color, style, text, etc.) should have:
- Overview of module purpose
- Key types/functions summary
- Usage example
- Cross-references to related modules

### Struct Documentation
Each public struct should have:
- Purpose description
- Fields documentation
- Example construction
- Common use cases

### Function Documentation
Each public function should have:
- Brief description
- Parameters explanation
- Return value
- Panics section (if applicable)
- Example usage
- Errors section (for Result returns)

### Code Examples
- All examples must compile (use ```rust or ```rust,ignore)
- Show common use cases
- Demonstrate error handling where relevant

## Files to Document
- [ ] src/lib.rs (crate overview)
- [ ] src/color.rs
- [ ] src/style.rs
- [ ] src/segment.rs
- [ ] src/text.rs
- [ ] src/markup/mod.rs
- [ ] src/measure.rs
- [ ] src/console.rs
- [ ] src/box.rs
- [ ] src/cells.rs
- [ ] src/renderables/*.rs

## Verification
- Run `cargo doc --no-deps` successfully
- No missing docs warnings with #![warn(missing_docs)]
- Examples in docs compile","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. cargo doc builds cleanly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-agw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:47.917432688-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-b7s","title":"EPIC: End-to-End Testing & Validation Infrastructure","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive end-to-end test infrastructure with detailed logging to ensure rich_rust works correctly as a complete system.

## Background
Unit tests verify individual components, but E2E tests verify the entire rendering pipeline works correctly from user input to terminal output. This is critical for a terminal rendering library where visual correctness matters.

## Why E2E Tests Are Critical
1. **Integration verification**: Components that pass unit tests may fail when combined
2. **Visual regression detection**: Catch rendering changes that affect appearance
3. **Performance regression detection**: Catch slowdowns in real-world scenarios
4. **Cross-platform validation**: Ensure consistent behavior across environments

## Scope

### Test Infrastructure
- Test harness with detailed logging (using tracing crate)
- Golden file comparison for visual output
- Performance regression detection
- CI integration with artifact preservation

### Test Scenarios
- Complete rendering pipeline tests
- Complex document rendering
- Edge case combinations
- Performance benchmarks as tests

### Logging Requirements
- Structured logging with tracing
- Test-specific log levels
- Timing information for each phase
- Memory usage tracking
- Diff output for failures

## Success Criteria
- All major user scenarios covered by E2E tests
- Detailed logs available for debugging failures
- CI runs tests and preserves artifacts
- Performance baselines established and monitored","acceptance_criteria":"E2E test suite operational. All scenarios pass. Logs are detailed and useful.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:43.32014929-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:43.32014929-05:00"}

 {"id":"rich_rust-vva","title":"Validate Table Data Structures against RICH_SPEC Section 9.1-9.3","description":"## Objective
Verify Table, Column, Row, Cell struct definitions match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.

## Background
This is the first of three focused Table validation tasks. This one covers data structure definitions only, not algorithms.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.1 Table Struct Fields
- [ ] columns: Vec<Column>
- [ ] rows: Vec<Row>
- [ ] title: Option<Text>
- [ ] caption: Option<Text>
- [ ] box_style: BoxChars
- [ ] border_style: Option<Style>
- [ ] header_style: Option<Style>
- [ ] row_styles: Vec<Style> (alternating)
- [ ] padding: PaddingDimensions
- [ ] collapse_padding: bool
- [ ] show_header: bool
- [ ] show_edge: bool
- [ ] show_lines: bool
- [ ] expand: bool

### 9.2 Column Struct Fields
- [ ] header: Option<Text>
- [ ] footer: Option<Text>
- [ ] style: Option<Style>
- [ ] justify: AlignMethod
- [ ] vertical: VerticalAlignMethod
- [ ] width: Option<usize>
- [ ] min_width: Option<usize>
- [ ] max_width: Option<usize>
- [ ] ratio: Option<usize>
- [ ] no_wrap: bool
- [ ] overflow: OverflowMethod

### 9.3 Row and Cell Structs
- [ ] Row: cells: Vec<Cell>, style: Option<Style>, end_section: bool
- [ ] Cell: content: Text (or renderable), style: Option<Style>, vertical_align: VerticalAlignMethod

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (lines 1-200 approximately)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.1-9.3","acceptance_criteria":"All struct fields present and correctly typed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:40.178218558-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:18:42.11060951-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:18:42.11060951-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Data Structures

### Summary
The Table, Column, Row, and Cell struct definitions are **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.1-9.3. Differences are minor design choices rather than spec violations.

---

### Column Struct (lines 28-55) ✅ ALIGNED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| header | Text | Text | ✅ |
| footer | Text | Text | ✅ |
| header_style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| footer_style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| justify | JustifyMethod | JustifyMethod | ✅ |
| vertical | VerticalAlignMethod | VerticalAlign | ✅ (name differs) |
| overflow | OverflowMethod | OverflowMethod | ✅ |
| width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| min_width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| max_width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| ratio | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| no_wrap | bool | bool | ✅ |
| _index | usize | - | ⚠️ Missing internal |
| _cells | Vec | - | ⚠️ Missing internal |

**Note**: Internal fields (_index, _cells) not implemented - cells stored differently.

---

### Row Struct (lines 213-222) ✅ ALIGNED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| end_section | bool | bool | ✅ |
| cells | - | Vec\<Cell\> | ⚠️ Extra |

**Note**: Impl stores cells in Row (not Column._cells). This is a valid design choice.

---

### Cell Struct (lines 176-182) ⚠️ SIMPLIFIED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| style | Style | Option\<Style\> | ⚠️ Made optional |
| renderable | Box\<dyn Renderable\> | Text | ⚠️ Simplified |
| vertical | VerticalAlignMethod | - | ⚠️ Missing |

**Impact**: Cell is simplified to Text-only content. Per-cell vertical alignment missing but rarely needed.

---

### Table Struct (lines 251-304) ✅ MOSTLY ALIGNED

All 24+ configuration fields present and correctly typed:
- columns, rows, title, caption ✅
- width, min_width, box_style, safe_box ✅
- padding, collapse_padding, pad_edge, expand ✅
- show_header, show_footer, show_edge, show_lines ✅
- leading, style, row_styles ✅
- header_style, footer_style, border_style ✅
- title_style, caption_style, title_justify, caption_justify ✅

Minor differences:
- cells: Not separate (stored in Row instead)
- box_style: \&'static BoxChars vs owned
- safe_box: bool vs Option\<bool\>
- highlight: Missing (used for alternating row highlighting)

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS

The implementation closely follows the spec. Differences are:
1. **Architecture choice**: Cells in Row vs Column._cells (reasonable)
2. **Simplification**: Cell uses Text instead of generic Renderable (acceptable)
3. **Missing highlight**: Could be added if needed","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-wfq","title":"Validate Tree Rendering Components","description":"Validate Tree components against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Tree struct - root node and recursive rendering
2. TreeNode struct - label, children, expanded state
3. TreeGuides enum - ASCII, ROUNDED, BOLD, DOUBLE guide styles

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Tree indentation calculation per nesting level
- [ ] TreeGuides character sets match Python Rich exactly
- [ ] Branch vs last-child guide selection logic
- [ ] Nested tree depth handling (deep recursion)
- [ ] Tree label styling propagation
- [ ] Empty tree edge case
- [ ] Single-node tree edge case
- [ ] Wide Unicode labels in tree nodes

TREE GUIDE CHARACTERS (verify exact match):
- ASCII: |, +-, `-
- ROUNDED: │, ├──, └──
- BOLD: ┃, ┣━━, ┗━━
- DOUBLE: ║, ╠══, ╚══

 {"id":"rich_rust-wph","title":"Create Benchmark Suite","description":"## Objective
Establish a comprehensive benchmark suite using criterion for measuring performance.

## Background
Before optimizing, we need reliable benchmarks to:
1. Establish baseline performance
2. Measure improvement from optimizations
3. Detect regressions in CI

## Benchmarks to Create

### Color Benchmarks
- [ ] Parse named color (cold cache)
- [ ] Parse named color (warm cache, after LRU implemented)
- [ ] Parse hex color
- [ ] Color downgrade (TrueColor -> Standard)

### Style Benchmarks
- [ ] Style::parse() simple
- [ ] Style::parse() complex
- [ ] Style::combine()
- [ ] Style::render_to_ansi()

### Text Benchmarks
- [ ] Text::render() short text
- [ ] Text::render() long text
- [ ] Text::wrap() narrow width
- [ ] Text::wrap() wide width
- [ ] Text::divide() with many spans

### Table Benchmarks
- [ ] Small table (3x3)
- [ ] Medium table (10x10)
- [ ] Large table (100x100)
- [ ] Table with styled cells
- [ ] Table width calculation only

### Cell Width Benchmarks
- [ ] cell_len() ASCII
- [ ] cell_len() mixed (ASCII + CJK)
- [ ] cell_len() emoji-heavy

### End-to-End Benchmarks
- [ ] Console::print() with markup
- [ ] Panel rendering
- [ ] Full page render (table + panel + text)

## Implementation
```rust
// benches/benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};

fn color_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    c.bench_function("parse_named_color", |b| {
        b.iter(|| Color::parse("red"))
    });
}

criterion_group!(benches, color_parsing);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Files to Create
- benches/benchmarks.rs
- Cargo.toml (add criterion dev-dependency)","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmark suite created. All major code paths covered. Results documented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-wph","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:06.83084456-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

- Python rich/progress_bar.py for reference
- Python rich/spinner.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:00.317920614-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:25:10.265841141-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:25:10.265841141-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Progress Components

### Key Finding: Phase 2+ Feature Implemented Early

**RICH_SPEC.md explicitly states**: "Progress bars | Separate concern, Phase 2+"

This means Progress components are **NOT in Phase 1 scope** and have no formal spec to validate against. The implementation is ahead of schedule.

---

### ProgressBar Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 197-612)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Width calculation | ✅ | Respects available space |
| Percentage display | ✅ | show_percentage option |
| ETA calculation | ✅ | Based on elapsed time |
| Speed (items/sec) | ✅ | show_speed option |
| Elapsed time | ✅ | show_elapsed option |
| Description | ✅ | Optional task name |
| Finished message | ✅ | Custom completion text |
| Brackets | ✅ | Optional [ ] around bar |
| Style support | ✅ | Separate styles for completed/remaining/pulse |

**BarStyle enum** (5 variants):
- Ascii: # and -
- Block: █ and ░ (default)
- Line: ━ and ─
- Dots: ● and ○
- Gradient: █ and ░ with ▓ pulse

---

### Spinner Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 66-195)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Frame cycling | ✅ | next_frame() advances |
| Stateless query | ✅ | current_frame() doesn't advance |
| Style support | ✅ | Applied to rendered segment |
| Custom frames | ✅ | custom() constructor |

**Preset spinners** (7 variants):
- dots: ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏
- line: ⎺⎻⎼⎽⎼⎻
- simple: |/-\
- bounce: ⠁⠂⠄⠂
- growing: ⣾⣽⣻⢿⡿⣟⣯⣷
- moon: 🌑🌒🌓🌔🌕🌖🌗🌘
- clock: 🕐🕑🕒🕓🕔🕕🕖🕗🕘🕙🕚🕛

---

### Unit Tests ✅ COMPREHENSIVE

20 tests covering:
- ProgressBar creation and updates
- Rendering with various options
- All bar styles
- Spinner frame cycling
- Duration formatting
- Progress clamping (0.0-1.0)

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS

**Status**: Implemented ahead of schedule (Phase 2+ feature)
**Quality**: Well-designed with builder pattern and comprehensive tests
**Recommendation**: No changes needed; ready for use

Note: Since there's no formal spec, validation was done against implementation quality and Python Rich patterns.","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-xbu","title":"Validate Columns Layout Algorithm","description":"Validate Columns layout component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Columns renders multiple renderables side-by-side with automatic width distribution.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Column width calculation algorithm
- [ ] Equal width distribution mode
- [ ] Ratio-based width distribution
- [ ] Expand mode (fill available width)
- [ ] Padding between columns
- [ ] Vertical alignment within columns (top/middle/bottom)
- [ ] Overflow handling when content exceeds column width
- [ ] Empty column handling
- [ ] Single column edge case
- [ ] Mixed-height content alignment

KEY ALGORITHMS:
1. Available width = console_width - (padding * (num_columns - 1))
2. Per-column width = available_width / num_columns (equal mode)
3. Per-column width = available_width * ratio / total_ratio (ratio mode)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/columns.rs
- Python rich/columns.py for reference","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:01.838957632-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:01.838957632-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
